text: I arrive as career switcher deciding whether this can replace another course. My past: I have bought online courses before and often quit when the path became vague after the first week.
Today I want to: Understand what I would learn, how long it takes, and whether there is proof it works.
My life goal is: Build a career that gives me independence without wasting money on empty promises.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

I get stuck while trying to reflect on arrival, so I stop waiting and try to recover.
I think: This looks aimed at parents and children, not someone trying to switch careers.
My reaction: The speed claims catch my attention, but they feel broad and unsupported on this screen.
Idea: Show a career-switcher path with duration, projects, outcomes, and proof before the main call to action.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month(s) to continue is not worth it unless the product clearly connects to my goal of career independence.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that this does not yet look like a serious replacement for a career course for me. Confidence: high.
